reason_of_clustering: These futures contracts are grouped because they all pertain to the live cattle market, providing price information for livestock at different future delivery dates. This clustering helps financial analysts and investors to compare performance across term structures and make strategic decisions based on expected market movements and seasonal trends.

These contracts are grouped together as they all relate to feeder cattle, which are younger and lighter cattle that will eventually be sold as live cattle. Investors and analysts cluster these to evaluate the cost and availability of these cattle at various future dates, which can impact the live cattle market and related investment decisions as they are key inputs in the cattle value chain.
